species: Dicer1 cKO male mice are infertile due to defective postmeiotic male germ cell differentiation.
tissue: we demonstrate that pericentric heterochromatin is expressed during mouse spermatogenesis to produce major satellite repeat (MSR) transcripts.
cell_type: the physiological role of DICER in maintenance of male fertility extends to the regulation of pericentric heterochromatin through direct targeting of MSR transcripts.
disease_state: Dicer1 cKO male mice are infertile due to defective postmeiotic male germ cell differentiation.
sample_source: we performed in situ hybridization on adult testis cryosections using a probe specifically detecting the forward transcript.
age: Elevated expression of MSR transcripts at 18 dpp cKO testes suggests that the defect in the regulation of MSR transcript levels takes place in pachytene spermatocytes...
sex: Male germ cells are known to transcribe their genome widely...
strain: The mice were of mixed genetic background (C57Bl/6J and SV129).
